channel.title: Twitter Timeline RSS
channel.description: 你的 Twitter Following Timeline（via opencli）

item.title: My next guest Ryan (VP @mercury) 2x'd his productivity by building a second brain in Claude Code tra...
item.description:
My next guest Ryan (VP @mercury) 2x'd his productivity by building a second brain in Claude Code trained on 5 years of work history.
It runs locally and gets better every day.
The diagram below looks complicated but I asked him to break down exactly how it works.
📌 Subscribe to get our full episode tmr: https://t.co/1C6pbWzPYn
--- @petergyang | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 0
item.author: @petergyang

item.title:
Interestingly the failure of Bali is one of LACK of regulation
Another extreme is Portugal where bu...
item.description:
Interestingly the failure of Bali is one of LACK of regulation
Another extreme is Portugal where building new homes is kept close to impossible, but as a benefit of that you have clean unrept nature without any of this
The balance would be somewhere in the middle of Bali's lack of regulation and Portuguese/European over-regulation
Sane regulation
https://t.co/IqrIt913h4
--- @levelsio | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 87
item.author: @levelsio

item.title:
// Survey on Multi-Agent Systems //
The paper traces the landscape from classical paradigms (consen...
item.description:
// Survey on Multi-Agent Systems //
The paper traces the landscape from classical paradigms (consensus, distributed control, swarm intelligence, cooperative learning) to foundation-model-enabled MAS (LLM-based planning, role specialization, task decomposition, multi-modal coordination).
It highlights the hard open problems that neither camp has solved on its own: scalability in heterogeneous systems, alignment across agent collectives, efficient knowledge transfer, and real-time adaptation.
Paper: https://t.co/FhDcRjn9Km
Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/LRnpZN7L4c
--- @dair_ai | ❤️ 5 | 🔁 2 | 💬 1 | 👁 352
item.author: @dair_ai

item.title: I don't get it, if it's on bypass permissions why is it still asking me for permissions? https://t.c...
item.author: @petergyang

item.title:
Why did I name my chief of staff Simon?
Great question!
- I wanted a name that was two syllables...
item.description:
Why did I name my chief of staff Simon?
Great question!
- I wanted a name that was two syllables long, so it was fast enough to say but long enough that it was easier to hear.
- I wanted a name that was very unlikely to be misspelled by Wispr Flow and Siri.
- I wanted a name that, if I whispered it, my dictation tools could still pick it up.
- I wanted a name that is not as common (ex: John, Sam, Sarah, Laura) so it’s less likely to come up in client meetings.
- I wanted a name that is not (yet) on my team. If I were to hire a Simon, I would change the name of my Chief of Staff (likely to Pascal).
- I wanted a name that didn't sound like something else, and although 'Simon' sounds a little bit like 'assignment', it hasn't been an issue thus far.
- I was going to name it something funny like Waffle, but then realized I probably wouldn't listen to its advice as openly.
--- @alliekmiller | ❤️ 1 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 343
item.author: @alliekmiller
